mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 2.0% signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rebalance into equities.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls below 2.0% and remains there for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.55% as of 2020-01-15 indicates easing stance
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window post-trigger.

mechanism_narrative: Rising monthly payroll employment (PAYEMS) above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market strength that sustains consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 152,383 thousand as of 2020-01-15 shows strong employment
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns show zero correlation with PAYEMS growth rate, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand that reduces earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors price in slower growth.
candidate_trigger: Both RSAFS and HOUST decline month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 527,994M and HOUST at 1,365K as of 2020-01-15
regime_hint: demand-side slowdown or early recession signal
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of dual-contraction triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 110 index points signals robust supply-side capacity utilization and manufacturing momentum, sustaining corporate profitability and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above 110.0 and maintains level for 1 month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 109.677 as of 2020-01-15 near expansion threshold
regime_hint: supply-side expansion with rising capacity utilization
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following INDPRO > 110 crossings are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint condition of low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS < 2.0%) and declining inflation (CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth < 0.2%) signals accommodative monetary policy with price stability, enabling equity valuations to expand and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 2.0% AND CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth < 0.2% for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.55% and CPIAUCSL at 258.501 as of 2020-01-15
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are not significantly higher in dual-accommodation regime than in single-easing regime (t-test p > 0.15), or interaction coefficient is not significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 4.0% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.0% and remains elevated for 1 month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.5% as of 2020-01-15 indicates tight labor market
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65% of instances when UNRATE > 4.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly above 2.5% annualized signals sustained economic expansion that supports corporate earnings and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19,121.112B as of 2020-01-15 reflects ongoing expansion
regime_hint: sustained economic expansion with above-trend growth
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns show zero correlation with GDPC1 growth rate, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in housing starts (HOUST) and real GDP growth (GDPC1) signals demand-side weakness in both residential investment and overall economic activity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as recession risk increases.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines month-over-month for 2 consecutive months AND GDPC1 growth falls below 2.0% annualized
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,365K and GDPC1 at 19,121.112B as of 2020-01-15
regime_hint: demand-side recession signal with housing and GDP weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of dual-weakness triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.
